behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: A real failure can occur in range parsing, reference resolution, and evaluator wiring together; testing SUM as a standalone function could miss those. No external OS/network/model dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook via the public engine API, set A1..A10 to numeric values, set B1 to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, trigger recalculation, and assert B1 equals the expected total.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This depends on multiple modules (parser, dependency graph, evaluator) working together across cells. A unit mock could hide propagation errors.
behaviors.test_description: Using the engine API, set A1=5, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`, recalc, and assert B1=10 and C1=11. Then change A1 and verify both dependent results update correctly.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a graph-level behavior across cells; a unit test of one function may miss interactions between dependency registration and evaluator execution order.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1+1` and B1=`=A1+1` in an in-memory workbook, run evaluation with a timeout guard, and assert both cells produce the circular-reference error type instead of hanging.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often come from interaction between table-range resolution and function semantics. Mocking the lookup table layer can hide index/range bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a table range in the workbook, evaluate `=VLOOKUP(key, range, col, FALSE)` for an existing key and a missing key, and assert exact returned value and expected not-found error.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Nested expression parsing plus conditional evaluation across cell inputs is the likely failure boundary. A pure unit over a simplified AST can miss parser/evaluator integration defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to values covering all branches (e.g., 5, 15, 25), evaluate the nested IF formula through the engine, and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, and `high` respectively.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on both function logic and cell-value retrieval/empty-cell semantics. Unit-only tests can miss how empties are represented by the engine.
behaviors.test_description: Set cells with text and empty values, evaluate CONCATENATE formulas that include separators, and assert the final string omits empty-cell artifacts while preserving separators for non-empty tokens.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet resolution exercises workbook, sheet namespace, and evaluator wiring. Mocking sheet lookup can hide real reference-resolution failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create two sheets in one workbook, set `Sheet2!A1`, set `Sheet1!B1` to `=Sheet2!A1+1`, recalc, and assert the expected value in `Sheet1!B1`.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Topological recomputation is an inter-module dependency-graph behavior; unit mocks can miss stale-cache or ordering bugs across the full recalculation pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1 -> B1/C1 -> D1), recalc, mutate A1, recalc again, and assert all downstream cells reflect the new value with no stale intermediate results.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness spans editor state, formula storage, dependency graph, and recomputation. Unit tests on the undo stack alone can miss incorrect dependent-value restoration.
behaviors.test_description: Set up dependent formulas, capture baseline formula text and computed values, edit one upstream formula and recalc, perform undo via public API, then assert original formula text and all dependent computed values are restored.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real serializer/deserializer plus filesystem I/O. In-memory mocks of persistence can hide binary format and round-trip precision defects.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with formulas and high-precision floating-point values, save to a temp file using the real binary writer, load it back with the real reader, and assert exact formula text plus value equality (or defined tolerance policy) after recalculation.
